commitments.description: Learner can reason about kleene star.

edges.rationale: Plus is often taught via star.

items.prompt: Regex `a`. Fill slot a with yes/no for string `a`. Fill slot b with yes/no for string `aa`.
Regex `b`. Fill slot a with yes/no for epsilon. Fill slot b with yes/no for string `b`.
Reminder: a literal matches exactly one symbol. Regex `a`. Fill slot a for `a`. Fill slot b for `b`.
Which option describes regex `ab`? choice_a: matches `a` or `b`. choice_b: matches only the two-character string `ab`. choice_c: matches any string starting with `a`. choice_d: matches epsilon.
Which string is matched by `ab` but not by `a|b`? choice_a: `a`. choice_b: `b`. choice_c: `ab`. choice_d: epsilon.
Reminder: concatenation means the symbols must appear in order. Which string matches `ab`? choice_a: `a`. choice_b: `ab`. choice_c: `b`. choice_d: `aa`.
Regex `a|b`. Fill slot a with yes/no for string `a`. Fill slot b with yes/no for string `ab`.
Regex `a|bc`. Fill slot a with yes/no for string `bc`. Fill slot b with yes/no for string `abc`.
Reminder: alternation means either branch can match. Regex `a|b`. Fill slot a for `b`. Fill slot b for `ab`.
Which statement about `a*` is correct? choice_a: it matches one or more `a`. choice_b: it matches epsilon, `a`, and `aa`. choice_c: it matches only epsilon. choice_d: it matches exactly one `a`.
Which string is matched by `a*` but not by `a+`? choice_a: `a`. choice_b: `aa`. choice_c: epsilon. choice_d: `aaa`.
Reminder: star allows zero or more repetitions. Which string matches `(ab)*`? choice_a: epsilon. choice_b: `aba`. choice_c: `a`. choice_d: `b`.
Regex `a+`. Fill slot a with yes/no for epsilon. Fill slot b with yes/no for string `a`.
Regex `a+`. Fill slot a with yes/no for string `aa`. Fill slot b with yes/no for epsilon.
Reminder: plus requires at least one repetition. Regex `(ab)+`. Fill slot a for `ab`. Fill slot b for epsilon.
Which regex matches `ab` and `ac`, but not `a`? choice_a: `ab|c`. choice_b: `a(b|c)`. choice_c: `(ab)|c`. choice_d: `a|bc`.
Which string is matched by `a(b|c)` but not by `ab|c`? choice_a: `ab`. choice_b: `c`. choice_c: `ac`. choice_d: epsilon.
Reminder: parentheses change scope before concatenation. Which regex matches `ab` or `c`, but not `ac`? choice_a: `a(b|c)`. choice_b: `ab|c`. choice_c: `a|bc`. choice_d: `a(bc)`.

axes.rationale: Generated variants must preserve language membership semantics.
Generator output may vary in surface form.

perturbation_axes.description: Swap star and plus while keeping the rest of the regex fixed.
Move parentheses to create a minimal-pair scope change.
